title: Sources: Collier back to Lynx on supermax deal
content: Napheesa Collier is signing a one-year, $1.4 million supermax contract to return to Minnesota, sources said.

title: Report: Penix, Njoku among targets used in fraud
content: A former Alabama defensive end is accused of using wigs and fake IDs to impersonate NFL players to secure nearly $20 million in fraudulent loans, with a report identifying Michael Penix Jr., David Njoku and Xavier McKinney as the targeted players.

title: Messick loses no-hit bid in 9th; Guardians hold on
content: Guardians rookie Parker Messick's bid for MLB's first no-hitter of 2026 was broken up in the ninth inning, but Cleveland held on for a 4-2 win over the Orioles on Thursday night.

title: Trout's latest blast caps 5-homer series in Bronx
content: Mike Trout homered for the fifth time during a four-game series at Yankee Stadium by crushing a 446-foot drive in the seventh inning.

title: Devils hire Panthers executive Mehta as GM
content: The Devils have hired Sunny Mehta, who was an assistant GM when the Panthers won the Stanley Cup the past two years, as their general manager.

title: Wings stifle Fudd-Bueckers relationship questions
content: The WNBA's Wings shut down a question to No. 1 overall draft pick Azzi Fudd about her relationship with teammate Paige Bueckers, which the two made public in 2025.

title: Sources: NWSL expected to vote on calendar shift
content: The NWSL's board of governors is expected to vote later this month on whether to flip the league's calendar to a fall-to-spring season, multiple sources told ESPN.

title: WR Knox found negligent in crash, to pay $2.8M
content: A Texas court on Wednesday issued a default judgment of more than $2.8 million against Theodore Knox, the co-defendant in one of the civil lawsuits against Chiefs receiver Rashee Rice stemming from a 2024 street racing crash in Dallas.

title: 👀 Inside the making of Mariners' Ichiro statue
content: You know about the reveal mishap. Here's what went into creating the sculpture that will forever honor a Seattle icon.

title: Stanley Cup contender flaws: Biggest issues for the NHL's top playoff teams
content: No NHL team is perfect, and postseason opponents will be certain to key in on these areas of weakness.

content: Greg Wyshynski catches you up on all of the storylines you might have missed entering this weekend's event.

title: WrestleMania 42: Key stats for all 13 matches across both nights
content: With eight title bouts in two nights, WrestleMania 42 is stacked. Get caught up before this weekend's PLE.

title: How all 32 teams can crush the NFL draft: We have prospects and positions to target
content: A week before the draft, Ben Solak hits the top priorities for every single team -- and the prospects who can meet those needs.

title: Out with the old, in with the new: Ravens unveil new uniforms
content: The Ravens revamped their on-field look since 1999 with new uniforms after a process that took three years.

title: 'A bigger shock to the system than we acknowledged': How 48 hours changed the Lakers' season
content: After a devastating blowout loss in Oklahoma City on April 2, the Lakers lost two of their three stars. Now they face Kevin Durant and the Houston Rockets -- as a home underdog.

title: How a 'big argument' and lucky draft moves led to the Chiefs selecting Chris Jones
content: A look back 10 years later at the selection of Jones, one of the most important picks in franchise history.

title: What makes Arizona QB Noah Fifita tick? Loyalty, generosity
content: Fifita, who runs monthly community events, is one of just five senior QBs nationally who has not transferred.

title: Semifinal predictions: Don't bet against Arsenal despite misfiring attack
content: The UEFA Champions League quarterfinals largely lived up to expectations, with three of four games going down to the wire. Now that the dust has settled, let's break down the semifinals and predict who advances.

title: UCL talking points: Slot's bizarre choices, Bayern now favorites?
content: Arne Slot's decision to start Alexander Isak backfired, badly. Our experts weigh in on that and other key moments.

title: LIV Golf is still going, but its days seem numbered and probably always were
content: LIV made a lot of noise, caused some nervous days at the PGA Tour and even forced some positive changes. But ultimately, it never did enough to make a real challenge.

title: MLB Power Rankings: NL teams dominate top 10 with two debuts
content: Three weeks into the season, two red-hot NL teams are shooting up our list while another powerhouse plummets.

title: Women's college basketball transfer rankings for 2026-27 season
content: Iowa-bound Dani Carnegie moves up to No. 4. Jada Williams, who is transferring to LSU, jumps to No. 6.

title: Verstappen finding joy in his Nürburgring side quest
content: Max Verstappen hasn't been very happy in F1 this year, but his Nürburgring 24 Hours side quest is reinvigorating the four-time world champion.

title: Mason Miller tops all pitchers? CJ Abrams goes 30-100-30? Don't be surprised
content: Eric Karabell looks into the crystal ball to tell fantasy baseball managers what might come to pass in the not-too-distant future.

content: Plan for the 2026 Fantasy Women's Basketball season with the top 100 rankings, including player position ranks.

title: Attack, attack, attack: Meet the team whose tactics have to be seen to be believed
content: NEC have the most attacking, aggressive style around, and it's working as they sit third in the Dutch Eredivisie and are into the final of the Dutch Cup.

title: Record-transfer Isak needs to step up for Liverpool after Ekitike injury
content: Just back from injury, Alexander Isak has to hit the ground running if Liverpool are to reach the Champions League.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Liverpool eye move for Bournemouth's Senesi
content: Liverpool could turn to Marcos Sensei as their new defender in the summer. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Alycia Baumgardner's journey to become the face of women's boxing
content: An early setback and a positive test -- since cleared -- didn't define Baumgardner. Now, she's on the doorstep of leading the sport.

title: Amravati case: How flat, cafe and hill station turned hellholes of sex abuse; 4 more nabbed
content: Police arrested four more suspects in the Amravati sexual abuse case, bringing the total to eight in custody. The main accused allegedly lured women and recorded sexual acts, with digital trails leading to the viral spread of videos. Investigations are ongoing to identify more victims and potential accomplices.

title: 'Broke down in tears': Artemis II crew recall 'otherworldly' experience of their trip to the Moon
content: Artemis II astronauts shared their historic Moon journey's impact, fostering global unity and profound personal bonds.  Commander Wiseman highlighted the crew's unbreakable connection, while mission specialist Koch was moved by the worldwide pride.  The crew also recounted the intense re-entry and the awe-inspiring view of Earth from deep space, emphasizing humanity's collective achievement.

title: Shreyas Iyer sums up Mumbai Indians' collapse in one line
content: Shreyas Iyer's astonishing boundary catch against Mumbai Indians drew disbelief from Rohit Sharma and Suryakumar Yadav. Punjab Kings secured a seven-wicket victory, with Iyer humorously crediting his athletic feat for pulling the team back into the game. Prabhsimran Singh also contributed with a fluent fifty.

title: Oil prices today: Crude falls in early trade on hopes of easing Middle East conflict
content: Oil prices dipped as hopes for a Middle East peace deal surged following a ceasefire and potential US-Iran talks. While a temporary agreement might be close, concerns over significant supply disruptions from the Strait of Hormuz closure persist. Experts anticipate continued price volatility until lasting peace and open trade routes are restored.

title: US expands its visa restriction policy; says: America will deny visa to adversary supporters
content: The US State Department has significantly expanded its visa restriction policy in the Western Hemisphere, targeting 26 individuals. This move aims to counter those working for US adversaries to undermine American interests, regional security, and democratic sovereignty. The administration asserts this action protects national security and promotes regional safety and prosperity.

title: Nitish Kumar Reddy Interview: ‘I have overdone things… now I realise that’
content: In an exclusive conversation with TimesofIndia.com, the all-rounder opens up on the need of not overdoing things, the mental toughness, awareness about his game and the dream of playing for India in 2027 ODI World Cup.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' fails to break 'Dhurandhar' record for highest week 4 collection
content: 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' continues its impressive box office journey, crossing Rs 1,100 crore in India and setting a new record for fourth-week collections. While earnings dipped on its 29th day, the film's overall performance remains stellar, securing the second-highest Week 4 earnings ever. Its global gross now stands at an astounding Rs 1,737.74 crore.

title: 'Iran ready to hand over uranium': Trump makes big claim; Tehran denies any deal reached
content: President Trump announced a potential breakthrough with Iran, stating a deal to end the conflict is "very close" and that Tehran has agreed to surrender its enriched uranium stockpile. Discussions are ongoing regarding a 20-year suspension of Iran's nuclear program, with potential talks held in Pakistan. Separately, Israel and Lebanon have agreed to a 10-day truce.

title: ‘Why didn’t you move?’: BJP MLA’s son rams SUV into pedestrians, 5 injured
content: An SUV allegedly driven by the son of a BJP MLA injured five people in Madhya Pradesh's Shivpuri district. The vehicle reportedly rammed into a motorcycle and two women walking on the road. Police have registered a case against the MLA's son, Dinesh Lodhi, and are investigating the incident.

title: Just weeks before big Siri launch, Apple may send dozens of engineers to bootcamp
content: Apple is sending Siri engineers to an AI coding bootcamp just weeks before WWDC 2026, where a revamped voice assistant is expected. This move highlights the Siri team's lag in adopting AI-assisted tools, unlike other Apple divisions.  The company's struggles with Siri's reliability and a significant deal with Google for AI models underscore the urgency to fix its voice assistant.

title: HAL, Trent & more: Top stocks on brokers' radar today
content: Analysts are bullish on Adani Enterprises, HAL, and Trent, citing growth drivers like new energy and engine co-production. Aurobindo Pharma's biologics expansion shows promise but financial gains are distant. Aditya Birla Capital's housing finance arm impresses with strong loan growth and improved asset quality, despite minor revenue misses.

title: Lightning-Canadiens playoff opener delayed over arena and broadcast conflicts
content: Playoff opener between the Montreal Canadiens and Tampa bay lightening has been postponed because of issues with arena availability and the broadcast schedule. The shift moves Game 1 to Sunday to give both teams more time to prepare. Having rested key players and having all logistics done, the next question is how the re-worked schedule would affect the early momentum in this much-hyped NHL game.

title: Dianna Russini’s divorce remarks resurface amid Mike Vrabel affair rumors
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, and Dianna Russini, the popular NFL insider, have millions fuming ever since their pictures from a luxurious hotel in Arizona went viral, a few days ago. While both Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini have denied affair rumors, the allegations refuse to die down. Amid all the mess, Dianna Russini has spoken about some issues in her marriage.

title: Aryna Sabalenka vs Coco Gauff: Who is the richer tennis icon
content: Aryna Sabalenka and Coco Gauff are two of the biggest stars in women’s tennis today. Both icons have won Grand Slam titles. They have ruled on the top of the WTA rankings. There is no doubt that they are among the highest earners in the sport. While Sabalenka dominates with power and consistency, Gauff shines with speed and confidence.

title: Noida protest: 2 Pakistani X handles incited unrest; 13 FIRs registered, 62 in custody
content: Noida police claim two Pakistan-operated X accounts, Anushi Tiwari and Meer Ilyas Inc, fanned violence during a labour protest by spreading false death claims. Authorities are also investigating the 'Mazdoor Bigul Dasta' group for allegedly mobilizing and inciting workers, with three key individuals identified and two arrested.

title: Vijay's 'Jana Nayagan' leak: Three more arrested by Chennai Cyber Crime police
content: Chennai's Cyber Crime police have made headlines by arresting nine individuals, which notably includes a freelance editor, amid the tumult surrounding the leak of the forthcoming film 'Jana Nayagan' featuring Vijay. This unauthorized release has stirred a wave of indignation, leading stars like Suriya and R Madhavan to passionately advocate for a boycott of pirated materials.

title: 0 wickets in 5 matches! What's really going wrong with Bumrah? - Explained
content: Star pacer Jasprit Bumrah is struggling in IPL 2026, having conceded 164 runs without a wicket in five matches. Former India cricketer Irfan Pathan suggests Bumrah is bowling too many slower deliveries, with his average speed dropping below 130 kmph. Pathan believes reducing the percentage of slower balls and increasing pace will improve his effectiveness.

title: IPL 2026: Dhoni set to be back in action next week
content: MS Dhoni's return to action is anticipated next week as his calf injury rehabilitation progresses well. He could feature in upcoming matches against Mumbai Indians or Gujarat Titans. Dhoni has been actively batting in nets, and if fully fit, may play as an 'Impact Player' with Sanju Samson continuing wicketkeeping duties.

title: ‘I don’t have much to say’: Pandya after MI sink to fourth straight defeat
content: Mumbai Indians face a tough challenge after a significant loss to Punjab Kings. Skipper Hardik Pandya acknowledges the team needs to find solutions.  Punjab Kings continue their winning streak, moving to the top of the standings.  Opener Prabhsimran Singh's performance is highlighted as a key factor in their success.

title: 'If deal is signed, I might go': Trump hints at Pak visit amid truce talks with Iran
content: President Trump indicated he might visit Pakistan if a US-Iran peace deal is finalized there, praising Islamabad's "constructive" role in facilitating talks. He expressed optimism about the negotiations, suggesting a breakthrough could be imminent and lead to lower oil prices. This follows recent discussions in Pakistan, with further talks likely to occur in the same location.

title: Vedanta blast: FIR filed against Anil Agrawal, plant head
content: Chhattisgarh police have filed an FIR against Vedanta Resources chairman Anil Agrawal and others following a deadly blast at their Sakti power plant. The incident, which claimed 20 lives, is attributed to serious operational lapses, including a rapid increase in boiler load and system failures. A special investigation team is probing the tragedy.

title: 'Had a very good talk': Trump on 40-min phone call with 'friend' PM Modi
content: President Donald Trump described his recent conversation with Prime Minister Narendra Modi as "very good," highlighting their strong friendship and India's progress. The leaders discussed bilateral cooperation and the critical situation in West Asia, emphasizing the need for a secure Strait of Hormuz. This marks their third call this year, following discussions on trade and regional stability.

title: Watch: Rohit Sharma, Suryakumar Yadav’s priceless reactions to Shreyas Iyer stunner
content: Punjab Kings skipper Shreyas Iyer stunned everyone with a sensational boundary catch to dismiss Hardik Pandya, leaving Rohit Sharma and Suryakumar Yadav in disbelief. Despite a brilliant unbeaten 112 from Quinton de Kock, Mumbai Indians fell short as Punjab Kings secured a convincing win, powered by half-centuries from Iyer and Prabhsimran Singh.

title: NFL trade rumors: Buffalo Bills could bring back former $12M defensive end
content: The Buffalo Bills have been a force to be reckoned with on the field in the last few seasons, with 12 wins and just 5 losses in 2025. While the Buffalo Bills were expected to break records in the last season, the team witnessed a heartbreaking loss against the Denver Broncos in the Divisional Round. Ahead of the new season, the team is focused on fixing its roster to bounce back on the field for the new season.

title: 'I have no regrets': Hansika Motwani breaks silence on divorce from Sohael Khaturiya
content: Hansika Motwani and businessman Sohael Khaturiya were granted a mutual consent divorce by Mumbai’s Bandra family court on March 11, 2026, bringing an end to their marriage of over three years. The couple, who tied the knot in December 2022, had been living separately since July 2024 due to irreconcilable differences. No alimony was sought by either party. While Hansika did not directly address the divorce, she recently spoke about her personal life and the scrutiny around it.

title: Israel-Lebanon agree to 10-day truce: What it means for Iran-US peace talks
content: US President Donald Trump has brokered a 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, inviting both leaders to the White House for crucial talks. This move aims to de-escalate regional tensions after months of heightened conflict. While battlefield clashes with Iran have eased, economic and strategic pressures persist, particularly around the Strait of Hormuz, keeping the region on edge.

title: Mirwaiz urges Centre to release Engineer Rashid, says MP’s dad critical
content: cleric says he visited jailed MP's father, Khazir Mohammad Sheikh, at SMHS hospital in Srinagar . he said the 'silent suffering' of political prisoners lodged in different jails across the country .

title: Census will list caste, says Amit Shah, rules out Muslim women's quota
content: samajwadi party MP Dharmendra Yadav says his party will never support women's reservation till separate provision for quota for OBC and Muslim women is made in the law . he reiterates govt's stand that the caste of citizens will be mentioned during the ongoing census to reject the claim that Centre's decision is driven by its unwillingness for the same .

title: Harivansh Narayan Singh set for 3rd term as RS deputy chair amid opposition boycott
content: the office of the deputy chairman, Rajya Sabha, fell vacant after Harivansh's term ended on April 9 . if elected, it will be his third term . the opposition has decided to boycott the election .

title: 'UCC not linked to religion, its enactment constitutional ambition', rules SC
content: supreme court agrees to examine validity of blatant discrimination of Muslim women in property inheritance under the 90-year-old Muslim Personal Law (Shariat) application Act . a bench of CJI Surya Kant and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and Vipul M Pancholi sought responses from law and minority affairs ministries on a PIL . the right of equal inheritance cannot be denied to 10 million Muslim women, both in intestate and testamentary succession .

title: ADR data reveals 20% candidates fighting Bengal polls are crorepatis
content: the poorest candidate, with assets of Rs 500, is from the new AJUP . 63 of the 148 nominees have pending criminal cases, with 48 in serious offences . the poll affidavits of 1,475 candidates were analysed by aDR .

content: vijay announced a slew of freebies in his election manifesto . ranging from a Rs 2,500 monthly dole for women under 60 years . most of the promises target youth and women .

title: Calcutta HC raps EC: Judge slams order on assistant professors as presiding officers
content: a group of assistant professors challenged EC's order directing them to join as presiding officers for the Bengal elections . they argued EC should not be drafted for polling duties without specific reasons to be recorded in writing by the DEO . a judge slams the election commission for not producing notifications .

title: India, Austria ink 12+ pacts on defence, counterterrorism & tech
content: the visit was the first by an Austrian chancellor in 42 years . the visit saw over a dozen agreements and announcements across counterterrorism, military affairs and technology . he said the world was going through a serious and tense situation .

title: J&K SIA files chargesheet against 10 in ‘white collar’ module case
content: 10 people are accused in the 'white collar' terror module case . one of them, Umar Un Nabi, was killed in the red Fort blast last Nov . the other nine are: arif Nisar Dar, Yasir Ul Ashraf Bhat, maqsood Ahmad Dar of Nowgam, Irfan Ahmad Wagay of Shopian, Zameer Ahmad Ahanger of Ganderbal, Muzamil Shakeel Ganaie of Pulwama

title: Nepal to probe assets of 7 former PMs, ex-king, 100s of ministers, bureaucrats
content: the move will bring under its lens former king Gyanendra Shah, three presidents, all heads of govt since 2005-06 . the move opens the widest scrutiny yet of the country's post-monarchy political and bureaucratic elite .

title: Keys to the offseason: Free agency and draft plans for every eliminated NHL team
content: the 2026 Stanley Cup playoffs are about to begin, meaning 16 teams are looking forward to their shot at the best trophy in sports . how should they modify their rosters this summer via trades, free agency and the draft? read on for full breakdown on every team that is no longer eligible for the Stanley Cup in 2026 .

title: Bengal elections: In Nandigram, BJP's Suvendu faces challenge from old loyalist
content: there are no BJP or Trinamool Congress flags anywhere . identity politics has arrived in Nandigram . Suvendu Adhikari defeated chief minister Mamata Banerjee in 2021 .

title: Biggest haters funeral: Chris Paul pokes fun at Clippers after play-in tournament loss
content: steph Curry drops 35 points with seven 3-pointers to help the Warriors defeat the Clippers . at least one ex-Clipper relished the Golden State Warriors ' season in the play-in tournament . he shared a viral meme of someone who posted they attended their "biggest haters" funeral .

title: 2027 SC Next ESPN 300 player rankings: College comps for the top prospects
content: we are looking at who has the chance to make the biggest impact as a freshman with a current college player he compares to favorably . he's big, athletic and raw, but steadily becoming more polished and will be ready in the fall of 2027 to challenge for snaps .

title: Are the Dodgers this good? Are the Mets this bad? What we learned from MLB's $1 billion series
content: the Los Angeles Dodgers and New York Mets have a combined payroll of more than $1 billion . but only one of them is getting their money's worth so far . the Mets fell to 7-12 and are in last place in the national league East .

title: Wetzel: For the good of WNBA, commish needs thicker skin
content: the WNBA has two new expansion franchises (Portland and Toronto) it has a new labor deal and its biggest and most widespread media rights deal ever . it has superstar A'ja Wilson seeking to lead her Las Vegas Aces to a fourth title in five years . veteran-led rosters in new york, Minnesota and elsewhere are eager to stop them .

title: Yates' final rankings for the draft: Sizing up the top 150 prospects
content: a week away from the 2026 draft, my prospect rankings are complete . my top 25 rankings were released in January, expanded the list to 50 in march . this ranking isn't representative of where I think players will ultimately be drafted .

title: Fantasy baseball news: Astros, Padres struggle to keep rotations healthy
content: fantasy baseball buzz file aims to provide fantasy managers with the intel they need as news breaks around baseball . the Pittsburgh Pirates host the Washington Nationals at 12:35 PM ET leads off today's abbreviated 10-game schedule . three evening affairs round out the action .

title: Top 10 tips to play and win ESPN fantasy women's basketball like the pros
content: the WNBA and the player's union negotiated a new collective bargaining agreement . there is opportunity for unprecedented player movement during the free agency window . we should start our discussion on ways to win at fantasy hoops this season .

title: Ex-Virginia Governor Shoots Wife Dead, Then Kills Self Amid Divorce Case
content: Justin Fairfax fatally shot his wife, a successful dentist, in the basement before going to an upstairs bedroom and killing himself, Davis said. Their son called 911.

title: "You Can Meet Me Easily": Vijay To Voters Amid Opposition's Criticism
content: Since launching his political party in 2024, one of the main criticisms against Vijay has been his alleged inaccessibility.

title: "Camel Dreams Of Cottonseed": Iran Mocks Trump's Big Peace Deal Claims
content: US President Donald Trump has said that Washington and Tehran were "very close" to a peace deal and insisted that Iran had agreed to hand over its enriched uranium, a key sticking point in negotiations.

title: Parliament Special Session LIVE: Lok Sabha Voting Today On Women's Reservation, Delimitation Bills
content: Parliament's 3-day special session Live Updates, Day 1: The government is expected to introduce three amendment Bills aimed at operationalising the legislation, which was passed in 2023 to provide 33% reservation for women in the Lok Sabha and State

title: "They Want Me": Trump Says He May Visit Pakistan If US, Iran Sign Deal
content: US President Donald Trump said on Thursday that if a deal with Iran to conclude the war is reached and signed in Islamabad, he might go, and that Iran has agreed to almost everything.

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: Lebanon Says Israel Violated Ceasefire, Calls It "Acts Of Aggression"
content: US-Iran War Live: The Iran war began when the US and Israel attacked Iran on February 28. Iran responded with its own strikes on Israel and Gulf states that host US bases.

title: "Pope Has To Understand This Is Real World": Trump On Threat From Iran
content: US President Donald Trump on Thursday said Pope Leo was free to say what he wanted but that it was important for him to understand that Iran could never have a nuclear weapon.

title: Bangladesh Court Grants Bail To Hindu Monk Chinmoy Das In One Case
content: A Bangladesh court has granted bail to Hindu monk Chinmoy Das in one case, while he remains in custody.

title: Case Against Vedanta Group Chairman After Blast Kills 20 In Chhattisgarh
content: The Chhattisgarh police on Thursday registered an FIR against Vedanta Group chairman Anil Agarwal and others in connection with the blast at the firm's power plant in Sakti district that claimed 20 lives, an official said.

title: "Nothing Against" Pope, But He Must Understand "Nasty World": Trump
content: US President Donald Trump said Thursday that Pope Leo XIV can say what he likes about world issues, but needs to understand the realities of a "nasty world."

title: 10-Day Ceasefire Deal Between Israel, Lebanon Takes Effect
content: A 10-day ceasefire deal agreed between Lebanon and Israel took effect on Thursday, as US President Donald Trump said he was trying to set up the first-ever meeting between the leaders of the two countries.

title: How Middle East War Exposed Weakness In British Military
content: When a British military base in Cyprus was hit by a drone early on in the Iran conflict in March, Britain, whose navy was the largest in the world at the start of World War Two, took three weeks to deploy one warship to the eastern Mediterranean.

title: "Friend Of Mine From India, He's Doing Great": Trump Praises PM Modi
content: US President Donald Trump on Thursday described Prime Minister Narendra Modi as a "friend" and said he had held a "very good talk" with the Indian leader.

title: Caste, Logistics, Digital Tools: How India Will Count 1.4 Billion People
content: More than 3 million officials, mostly school teachers, will physically visit every house, knock on every door and count every single person living in the country, across its 36 states and federal territories.

title: US Says Lebanon Commits To Stopping Hezbollah Attacks On Israel
content: The US State Department said that the ceasefire reached between Israel and Lebanon on Thursday entails a commitment by Beirut to prevent any Hezbollah attacks.

title: Congress Suspends 5 MLAs Who Cross-Voted In Haryana Rajya Sabha Polls
content: The party's Disciplinary Committee had issued show-cause notices to the MLAs. Only two of the five MLAs appeared before the panel to submit their response.

title: Over 10 Lakh Travelled To India Since Start Of Middle East Tensions: Centre
content: The government continues to take necessary steps to ensure preparedness and smooth functioning across key sectors amid the evolving situation in West Asia, the Ministry of Petroleum & Natural Gas said.

title: Annatto seed growers pitch for government support
content: Cultivated on nearly 10,000 acres, these seeds are the world’s second most important natural colourant after caramel. Thanks to impressive laboratory tests confirming seed quality and organic farming practices, they are in high demand across the European Union and the Middle East. However, the absence of government-backed organic certification, a proper procurement mechanism, and adequate storage facilities is forcing farmers to sell their produce at throwaway prices to middlemen, writes T. Appalanaidu.

title: Inside Andhra’s millet push that is turning women into entrepreneurs
content: Across Andhra Pradesh, a government initiative is building women-led millet enterprises that link farms, kitchens and local markets into viable livelihoods

title: Assembly Elections 2026 LIVE: ECI begins EVM-VVPAT commissioning for Tamil Nadu and West Bengal phase-1 polls
content: The commissioning began in the presence of the candidates or their authorised representatives as well as General Observers, according to the release

title: Mining push meets tribal resistance
content: Tribal villagers and the administration are at loggerheads over bauxite mining at the Sijimali hill, which straddles Rayagada and Kalahandi districts in south Odisha. Tension has gripped the region ever since attempts were made to lay a road to a bauxite block. On April 7, police fired tear gas shells to disperse protesting villagers and several personnel were injured in the counter-attack. Satyasundar Barik explains why it is not an isolated incident

title: TCS Nashik case: Plea in Supreme Court seeks directions to declare forced religious conversion as 'terrorist act'
content: The petition filed by advocate Ashwini Kumar Upadhyay contended that deceitful religious conversion is not only a serious threat to sovereignty, secularism, democracy and liberty but also a menace to fraternity, dignity, unity and national integration

title: Delimitation: In data and numbers
content: A look into how 2011 Census-based delimitation could impact India’s political landscape, parliamentary representation and electoral dynamics

title: Billionaire Anil Agrawal named in Sakti power plant explosion FIR
content: On April 14, a tube carrying high-pressure steam from the boiler to the turbine exploded at Vedanta’s power plant in Singhitarai village killing 20 and injuring 16.

title: Madhya Pradesh authorities deploy force at illegal sand mining locations; take preventive measures
content: Morena SP Samir Sourabh said that 50% personnel from the SAF company have been deployed at Rajghat, which he described as the most crucial location related to illegal sand mining

title: Allahabad High Court declines to quash FIR against schoolgirls booked under U.P.’s anti-conversion Act
content: In her complaint to the police, the complainant described an incident in December 2025 when she was allegedly forced to wear the veil and later taken to a restaurant by the accused girls

title: Thanjavur Big Temple inscriptions bring alive the fierce fight between Shivaji and Afzal Khan
content: The inscriptions, written in Marathi on the south-western walls near the Vinayagar Temple, also describe the history of the Maratha kings and praise Danish missionary Rev. Friedrich Christian Schwartz. They also highlight how Serfoji sent his army and offered financial assistance to the British during the war against the Marudu brothers

title: Alliterative expression as the Dravidian movement’s political weapon in Tamil Nadu
content: Alliterative slogans shaped Tamil Nadu politics, with leaders like Annadurai and Karunanidhi using them to connect with the masses and win support

title: More women in the fray, but Tamil Nadu polls remain overwhelmingly male
content: From just 11 women candidates in 1967, accounting for 1.41% of the total number of candidates, the figure has risen to 443 in 2026, making up 11.01% of the contestants. Though the increase is notable, proportionally, women continue to occupy only a small share of the candidate pool

title: J&K Police file charge sheet against 10 accused part of ‘doctors’ terror module’
content: The accused formed a highly clandestine module, actively engaged in radicalisation, recruitment, and operational preparations for carrying out terrorist attacks across the country, say police

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Vijay releases TVK manifesto; criticises Dravidian majors’ poll promises
content: He asserts that his party has only promised what was possible, practical, and implementable if voted to power

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Spat between Centre and State takes a toll on quality of higher education
content: A total of 16 universities in T.N. continue to suffer without institutional heads, even as the hearing on a bill on giving power to the State Government to appoint V-Cs is on before the High Court

title: Women MPs welcome Women’s Reservation Bill, against its ‘weaponisation’
content: While endorsing their party line, women legislators also cite the positive role of male founders and leaders of their parties in enabling the participation of women in jobs, education, and politics

title: T.N. election 2026: West Asia conflict eclipses election debates among Muslims in Ramanathapuram
content: Iran draws near-universal sympathy in Ramanathapuram, though the Muslim population is largely not Shia

title: Interesting battle on the cards in Madurai Central constituency
content: With Palanivel Thiaga Rajan, popular among the people in the constituency and a popular personality like Sundar. C, entering into the mix, the fight in the Madurai Central constituency has gathered much attention.

title: West Bengal SIR: Voters cleared by tribunals by April 21 or 27 will be able to vote in Assembly polls, says Supreme Court
content: The order by a Bench headed by Chief Justice of India Surya Kant spelt a huge reprieve for citizens whose right to vote was denied in the SIR under the “logical discrepancy” category

title: Delimitation won’t bring down south’s share of seats, Modi, Shah tell Lok Sabha
content: Amit Shah says those spreading ‘misconceptions... do not understand’; he tells Lok Sabha that seats of five southern States will increase from 129 to 195 and reassures people of Tamil Nadu that their ‘power is increasing’

title: T.N. election 2026: Cases filed in Madras High Court over assets of Vijay, Tamilisai Soundararajan
content: Litigants have sought an inquiry by Director General of Income Tax (Investigation) as it had been sought in another case filed against Deputy Chief Minister Udhayanidhi Stalin

title: High Court dismisses Karti Chidambaram’s writ for speedy disposal of plea to de-freeze salary account
content: Justices S.M. Subramaniam and K. Surender refuse to issue any such direction to the National Company Law Tribunal

title: Salem C. Vijiaraghavachariar, the freedom fighter who fought against Kala Paani
content: Salem C. Vijiaraghavachariar, one of the doyens of the national movement during the pre-Gandhi era and the first Tamil to become the All India Congress Committee president, was ordered to be transported to Andamans to serve a 10-year imprisonment. He appealed to the High Court of Madras and got his conviction quashed on January 9, 1883

title: How Scheduled Castes in Tirunelveli empowered themselves by establishing schools
content: The community established schools not only in Tirunelveli district but also in other parts of Tamil Nadu. Now, there are 28 schools originally established by the community that are functioning as government-aided primary schools in Tirunelveli

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: How a stool makes a huge difference at polling booth
content: Members of Tamil Nadu Dwarfism Association have reiterated a set a demands they have always made when the hustings is round the corner

title: Cases against DMK, AIADMK candidates in Madras High Court over alleged distribution of ₹8,000 coupons and ₹10,000 tokens
content: Madras High Court Chief Justice Sushrut Arvind Dharmadhikari and Justice G. Arul Murugan agree to hear both the cases on Friday

title: Parliament special sitting updates: Amit Shah says all elections till 2029 to be held based on existing number of seats
content: The law of the Delimitation Commission is exactly according to the old law. No changes have been made in it. The ongoing elections will not be affected by this, says Union Home Minister Amit Shah

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Cleanliness, safety, housing top agenda of many women candidates in Chennai
content: NTK’s Ayesha says traffic congestion has to be addressed immediately in Triplicane; AIADMK’s Gokula Indira seeks increased patrol for women’s safety and designated spaces for food carts

title: With BDS student’s death in Kannur, caste bias back in focus
content: The death of a student of Kannur Dental College has raised disturbing questions about caste discrimination on Kerala’s college campuses. The college has dismissed a faculty member and suspended another, pending inquiry. The incident led to an outpouring of related complaints from other students. The bereaved family, the student’s college-mates, faculty, and activists express their views

title: Women’s Reservation Act 2023 comes into force: Gazette notification
content: It is not immediately known why the act was notified in the midst of the debate in Parliament to amend the same law; Congress terms the move “absolutely bizarre”

title: Two held in U.P.’s Bijnor over 'anti-national' activities, separate cases; foreign links probed
content: Circle Officer Anjani Kumar Chaturvedi said Sameer was arrested from Najibabad for allegedly maintaining contact with individuals based in Saudi Arabia and South Africa, who were attempting to recruit youths in India for terror-related activities

title: Delimitation will turn Tamils into second-class citizens, says CM Stalin
content: There can never be a Prime Minister from the South if the Delimitation Bill goes through in Parliament, he says. Mr. Stalin also criticises the AIADMK general secretary, Edappadi K. Palaniswami, for claiming that the Chief Minister’s rejection of the Bill reflected his opposition to women’s reservation

title: Tamil Nadu registers 99.92% pass rate in CBSE Class 10 exams; most students gear up for second exam
content: Students are unhappy with their maths scores as the paper was difficult, most students are now preparing to take up the second exam for math

title: Residents demand boost for small industries and water supply for agriculture in Jolarpet
content: As per estimates, around 40,000 persons, mostly women, were directly engaged in 550 clusters of incense stick-making units

title: MPs of Left parties question delimitation, back women’s reservation
content: The Union government is committing an injustice towards States that have performed well in population control, Left parties say

title: Weaker rupee makes India 6th largest economy: IMF data
content: Determine the monthly installment amount for a loan Estimate the returns on investments made through SIPs Find out maturity amount and interest earned on PPF Check maturity amount and interest earned on an FD Estimate the pension amount and corpus accumulated under NPS A Mutual Fund Calculator helps estimate the future value of investments

title: Representation of southern States will not go down, a misconception is being spread: Shah
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah says “a major narrative is being built... a misconception is being spread” that the southern States will lose seats in the Lok Sabha due to delimitation

title: Inside the end of the Ja Morant era in Memphis
content: JA MORANT closed within an arm's length and launched off two feet in the baby blue paint . the 6-foot-8 Dyson Daniels took off for a two-handed dunk . despite a 5-inch height disadvantage, Morant managed to swipe the ball from behind .
